---
title: "Creating & Using Skills"
---

::: {.topic-badges}
[Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} · **Technical & Agentic** track · ~1 hour, self-paced
:::

[← Back to the Technical & Agentic track](technical-agentic.qmd){.track-back}

*Turn a prompt that works into a reusable, shareable skill your team can run again and again.*

## What you'll be able to do

- Write a reusable skill: job, steps, output, and guardrails
- Reuse it across tasks and share it with colleagues
- Know when a skill beats a one-off prompt

## Overview

A skill is a saved package of instructions for one job done well — "turn notes into our standard recap." Write it once; the assistant applies it whenever that job comes up. Claude has a formal Skills feature; in ChatGPT Edu, custom GPTs and project instructions do the same.

Most quality lives in the instructions, not the model. A good skill names the job, the steps, the output format, and the guardrails.

## Practice activities

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 1 · Moderate — Write a reusable skill</summary>
```

**Time** ~18 min · **Tools** ChatGPT Edu (custom GPT) or Claude

**Goal.** Package a repeated task as a shareable, reliable skill.

**Setup.** Pick a task you repeat — e.g., *“turn messy meeting notes into our standard recap.”*

**Steps.**

1. Draft the skill:

   > Help me write a reusable “skill” for this task. Include the job, step-by-step instructions, the exact output format, and guardrails (what it must never do). Task: [task].

2. Save it as a custom GPT’s instructions (or a Claude skill).

3. Test on two different inputs; tighten wherever it drifts.

**Expected result.** A documented skill that produces consistent output across different inputs.

**Check your work.** Run it on a deliberately tricky input — does it hold the format and the guardrails?

**Common pitfalls.** Most of the quality is in the instructions, not the model. Keep sensitive data out of skill files; share inside Drexel only.

**Stretch (optional).** Add a one-line “use this when…” and have a colleague test it cold.

```{=html}
</details>
```

```{=html}
<details class="activity"><summary>Activity 2 · Moderate — Start a small skill library</summary>
```

**Time** ~12 min · **Tools** a shared doc (Teams/SharePoint)

**Goal.** Begin a reusable team library so good prompts become shared tools.

**Setup.** A shared document the team can reach.

**Steps.**

1. Add 2–3 skills, each with a title, the instructions, an example input/output, and “use when.”

2. Review the set:

   > Review my skill library for overlap or gaps, and suggest one more skill this set implies we need.

3. Agree where it lives and that anyone can add.

**Expected result.** A starter library of 2–3 skills plus one suggested addition.

**Check your work.** Have a colleague run one skill cold — if they can’t, the instructions need work.

**Common pitfalls.** A skill only its author can run isn’t reusable. Document clearly.

**Stretch (optional).** Convert your best prompt from another workshop into a skill.

```{=html}
</details>
```


## Check your readiness

Answer these, then check — your score suggests whether to dive in or skim the readings first.

<div class="readiness-quiz">
<script type="application/json" class="quiz-data">
{"questions": [{"q": "Most of a skill's quality comes from…", "options": ["Clear instructions", "A bigger model", "More examples", "Longer prompts"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Instructions carry the quality."}, {"q": "A skill is worth writing when a task is…", "options": ["Repeated and worth standardizing", "One-off", "Trivial", "Secret"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Reuse is the payoff."}, {"q": "A good skill includes…", "options": ["Job, steps, output, and guardrails", "Just a title", "Only an example", "A password"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Those four parts make it reliable and shareable."}]}
</script>
</div>

## Recommended readings

Available in the shared OneDrive folder **Staff Faculty AI Workshop → Readings**, and online where linked:

- [**The Complete Guide to Building Skills for Claude**](https://resources.anthropic.com/hubfs/The-Complete-Guide-to-Building-Skill-for-Claude.pdf) — how to structure a reusable skill.
- [**AI Agent Skills: Why Skill Curation Is the Next Bottleneck**](https://turingpost.substack.com/p/fod152-ai-agent-skills-why-skill) — keeping a useful library of skills.

## Useful resources

- [Claude Skills (docs)](https://docs.claude.com) — Claude's formal Skills feature.
- [ChatGPT Edu (Drexel AI Tools)](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools) — the approved tool, with data rules.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data rules
Keep sensitive data out of skill instructions and example files; share **inside Drexel** only. See [Tools & Access](../tools.qmd) and Drexel's [AI Tools page](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools).
:::
